title: Check bit
hints: Use bitwise AND to check if the B-th bit is set: A & (1 << B).
If the result is non-zero, the bit is set, return 1.
If the result is zero, the bit is unset, return 0.
Remember: bit positions are 0-indexed, so the LSB is at position 0.
The expression (1 << B) creates a mask with only the B-th bit set.
